meta.name: raft
meta.description: Raft (raft.build) — neo-brutalist landing for a human+AI-agent chat workspace: hard black borders and offset shadows, Space Grotesk/Space Mono, candy accent palette on white, playful sticker rotation and float animations.

color.palette_type: candy multi-accent on monochrome ink — black-on-white base with a rotating set of flat saturated accents

primary.role: brand yellow (--primary-400 = brutal-yellow): logo tiles, highlight blocks, icon chips

secondary.role: ink: text, 2px borders, and all offset shadows (near-black with warm cast)

accent.role: brutal-pink (--accent-400): primary CTA fill ('Get started'), key highlights

neutral.usage: white page ground dominates; brutal-cream #fffaef tints select sections; warm 'brutal-stone' oklch ramp for muted text and quiet fills; black/45 for mono eyebrow labels

surface.elevated: #ffffff (elevation via hard offset shadow, not tint)

color.contrast_strategy: maximum: pure black ink and 2px black borders on white/candy fills; every accent used at full flat saturation, never as a tint gradient. Supporting accents: cyan #27ccf3, lime #a9d877, orange #f8a16f, red #f97264, lavender #bbafe6. Dark-mode token set exists (layer-page oklch 14.5%)

typography.font_style_notes: Geometric-quirky grotesque pairing from the same family: Space Grotesk bold for nearly everything (104 uses of font-display), Space Mono bold uppercase for eyebrows, badges, and chat metadata. Weight ramp reaches 900 (font-weight-black) for stickers. No serif anywhere.

spacing.content_density: comfortable — brutalist elements are chunky but sections breathe
spacing.section_rhythm: uniform vertical bands on white; section intros centered with mono eyebrow + bold Space Grotesk headline

layout.grid_system: centered container, CSS-grid feature card rows
layout.columns: 3-col feature cards, 2–3 col testimonial masonry, single centered hero
layout.alignment_tendency: centered hero and section heads; left-aligned card content; scattered rotated stickers break the grid deliberately

border_radius.large: 0 (token scale sm 4px / md 6px / lg 8px exists but marketing chrome stays square)
border_radius.pill: not used; avatars are square tiles

shape.border_usage: bold 2px solid black borders on everything interactive: buttons, cards, avatar tiles, badges, chat window
shape.divider_style: 2px black rules; sections otherwise separated by whitespace

elevation.shadow_style: hard offset, zero blur — the neo-brutalist paper-cutout stack
elevation.depth_cues: offset shadows + physical press: active state translates +1px/+1px and shrinks shadow to 1px 1px 0, as if pushing the element into the page; hover lifts -1px

iconography.style: small outlined icons at 1.5–2px stroke inside square colored chips with 2px black borders
iconography.preferred_set: in-house/lucide-style strokes; icons sit on brutal-yellow/cyan tiles

motion.easing: ease-in-out for ambient float; snappy linear-ish 100ms for interaction

duration_scale.normal: n/a — jumps straight from micro to ambient

motion.entrance_pattern: elements mostly render static; ambient float (translateY 0→-6px→0, 4s/6s infinite) on stickers
motion.philosophy: toy-like physicality: hover lifts and grows shadow, click physically depresses; decorative stickers bob and stay rotated (rotate-3/6/12)

components.button_style: square, 2px black border, flat candy fill (pink CTA, white secondary), bold Space Grotesk label, hard shadow; hover -1px lift, active +1px press with shadow collapse
components.card_style: white square cards with 2px black border and 4px offset shadow; icon chip top-left on colored tile
components.navigation_pattern: simple top bar with mono language switcher; links plain bold text
components.modal_style: square panel, 2px border, brutal shadow (inferred from token set)
components.list_style: chat-message lists inside a faux Slack-like channel window with square avatar tiles
components.component_notes: Signature component is the hero chat window: an 'engineering #42' channel where human and agent avatars (square, 2px border, on cyan/yellow tiles) exchange timestamped messages. Badges are square mono-font chips. Trusted-logos strip runs as an infinite marquee.

aesthetic.visual_metaphor: paper cutouts and stickers on a zine page; 'millennial web chat nostalgia' (a testimonial's own words)

visual_language.complexity: moderate — simple flat shapes, but many accent colors and scattered stickers
visual_language.ornamentation: decorative: rotated floating stickers, colored icon tiles, marquee, candy badges
visual_language.whitespace_usage: generous white ground keeps the loud elements readable
visual_language.visual_weight_distribution: chunky bordered blocks distributed evenly; rotation breaks monotony
visual_language.focal_strategy: hero statement + live chat-window demo as proof, then distributed card interest
visual_language.contrast_level: maximum — pure black on white and full-saturation fills
visual_language.texture_usage: none; everything is flat vector color

composition.hierarchy_method: weight and border/shadow chunkiness; color marks interactivity
composition.balance_type: symmetric skeleton with asymmetric sticker accents (rotate-3/6/12)
composition.flow_direction: vertical scroll, single column of bands
composition.grouping_strategy: hard 2px borders group everything explicitly; no subtle proximity grouping
composition.negative_space_role: white paper ground that stickers and cards sit on

imagery.photo_treatment: candid, deliberately unpolished personal photos in square tiles — team includes a white cat ('dd-cat') and casual iPhone selfies; one testimonial avatar is an anime-style illustration; all object-cover in square 2px-bordered frames
imagery.illustration_style: no illustration system; the logo is a hand-drawn-feel angular raft mark
imagery.graphic_elements: rotated sticker badges, square icon chips on candy fills, marquee logo strip, faux chat window
imagery.image_shape: squares only — square avatars, square cards, square chips; zero border-radius

interaction_feel.feedback_style: physical and immediate: lift on hover, depress on click
interaction_feel.loading_style: cursor-wait states on buttons; opacity holds
interaction_feel.microinteraction_density: medium — every interactive element has the lift/press behavior; ambient float on decor

brand_voice_in_ui.tone: playful direct: 'Build with Raft. Build with fun.'
brand_voice_in_ui.empty_state_approach: chat-metaphor placeholder copy (inferred from product framing)

overview.fallback_strategy: all effects are CSS keyframes; degrade to static with reduced motion

background_effects.description: flat white ground with occasional brutal-cream section tint; no gradients anywhere — flat color is the point

scroll_triggered_animations.trigger_points: none — content is static on scroll

text_effects.description: headline emphasis via flat color blocks/stickers behind or beside text, not text animation

image_effects.description: photos sit static in square bordered tiles

glassmorphism_neumorphism.style: none — antithetical to the aesthetic; all surfaces opaque and flat

visual_effects.composite_notes: Three CSS keyframes carry all motion: float (translateY 0/-6px/0, 4s and 6s variants) bobbing the rotated hero stickers, trusted-logo-marquee for the infinite customer strip, and pulse. The defining 'effect' is really the interaction model — hard offset shadows (2/4/6px, zero blur, #141111) that grow on hover (-1px lift) and collapse to 1px on press (+1px translate), making every element feel like a physical button. No canvas, WebGL, or animation libraries.
